description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular agricultural building with a prominent gable roof covered in corrugated metal sheets, bordered by wide, tan-colored eaves. The structure features a black and white exterior with large open bays on the longer sides, likely for ventilation and animal access. The roof is supported by visible beams and has a slightly weathered appearance, suggesting utilitarian use. The interior appears spacious and open, with ample headroom and clear access points. There are no visible doors, but the large open sections on the sides serve as entryways. The overall design is functional, prioritizing airflow and ease of movement for livestock.
description: A rectangular cowshed with a gable roof, wide tan eaves, and large open bays on the sides for ventilation and animal access. The exterior is black and white, with a utilitarian, spacious interior.
description_view_0: Front gable end with three large open bays and a clear view of the tan eaves and corrugated metal roof.
description_view_1: Side view showing the full length of the building, the wide eaves, and multiple rectangular openings along the black and white wall.
description_view_2: Rear gable end with several open bays and a clear view of the roof's structure and eaves.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with large rectangular wall openings and the full expanse of the corrugated roof.
attribute_door_style: no visible doors; large open bays serve as entry points
attribute_window_presence: no traditional windows; large open bays on both long sides
